name: Roslyn Semantic Model
summary: Typed, name-resolved view layered on top of Roslyn's syntax tree. Computed lazily — querying for one expression doesn't trigger full-program analysis. The model exposes type, symbol, and binding facts on demand by composing with the underlying red-green tree.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Roslyn's "semantic model" is the typed, name-resolved view layered on top of the syntax tree. Where the syntax tree is purely lexical, the semantic model answers questions: "what type does this expression have?" "what symbol does this identifier refer to?" "is this method override valid?" The model is computed lazily — querying it for one expression doesn't trigger full-program analysis.

sections.title: Lazy query model
sections.content:
Distinct from a separate IR layer: Roslyn does not lower the syntax tree into a different data structure for semantic queries. Instead, the syntax tree is queried *with* a semantic model parameter, and the model produces type/symbol facts on demand. This is the lazy-query equivalent of Salsa, but framed as IDE infrastructure rather than compiler architecture.

The pattern lets IDE features like hover-typing, go-to-definition, and find-references work at interactive latency without invalidating the entire compilation each time the user moves the cursor.
